religions.name: Church of the Last Signal
religions.doctrine: God spoke once through the towers and went silent; the faithful keep antennas tuned for the second broadcast.

ritual.name: The Tuning
ritual.text: #congregation# stand among dead satellite dishes, humming a carrier tone.

greeting: The dish sees you. Be received.
rumor: They say the Receivers heard something over {neighborhood} last #weekday#.
A Static Deacon bought every radio on #street#. Paid in cash, tested none of them.

boon.name: White Noise
boon.desc: You move quieter near humming electronics.

religions.name: Congregation of the Patient Tide
religions.doctrine: The water that took the streets was a first visitation. The faithful keep their ground floors empty and their accounts settled.

ritual.name: The Sounding
ritual.text: #congregation# wade to the knees at first light, reading the harbor's mood.

greeting: The tide finds every door, friend. Is yours open?
Settled your debts? It rises regardless.
rumor: The Tide people walked {neighborhood}'s old waterline again, singing the depth numbers.
A Sounder predicted the last surge to the hour. The harbormaster pays her now.

boon.name: Tide Sense
boon.desc: Weather and water never surprise you.

religions.obligation: Keep no possessions below the waterline.

religions.name: Order of the Open Ledger
religions.doctrine: The Crash was a judgment on hidden numbers. Salvation is an honest balance sheet, posted where anyone can read it.
tenets: A hidden number is a lie

ritual.name: The Posting
ritual.text: #congregation# chalk their week's earnings and debts on the public board, witnessed.

greeting: Your balance is between you and the board, friend.
The Ledger sees all accounts. Even yours.
rumor: The Tellers forgave every debt on #street# last #weekday#. Nobody knows whose money settled it.
A Reconciler audited a {faction} book-keeper. The book-keeper has not been seen.

boon.name: Fair Price
boon.desc: Merchants find it hard to cheat you.

religions.obligation: Post your net worth publicly each week.

religions.name: Shepherds of the Third Rail
religions.doctrine: The tunnels are the city's veins and the trains its breath; every closed station is a held breath that must someday be released.

ritual.name: The Headway
ritual.text: #congregation# descend with lanterns to sweep a dead platform and call out phantom departures.

greeting: Which line carried you here? All lines are one line.
rumor: The Shepherds lit the dead {neighborhood} station again. You could see the glow through the grates.
A Conductor sells maps of tunnels that aren't on any map. They're accurate.

boon.name: Tunnel Grace
boon.desc: Underground, you are faster and harder to follow.

religions.obligation: Never ride without paying respects at the platform's end.

religions.name: The Unbroken Lease
religions.doctrine: Home is a covenant older than any deed. A family kept in its rooms is the only sacrament; an eviction is the only sin.
tenets: The door belongs to who answers it
No empty building while one sleeper is cold

ritual.name: The Standing
ritual.text: #congregation# link arms on a stoop where the marshals are due, singing the address aloud.

greeting: Doors open for the faithful. Locks are for landlords.
rumor: The Lease moved a family into the empty tower on #street# overnight. Furniture and all.
An Advocate read a landlord his own paperwork until he wept and signed.

boon.name: Open Doors
boon.desc: Sympathetic tenants will hide you when heat is high.

religions.obligation: Answer any knock for sanctuary, whoever knocks.

religions.name: Saints of the Scoreboard
religions.doctrine: The game is the last honest oracle. The score cannot be argued with, and so the score is holy.
tenets: Every champion is a prophet briefly

ritual.name: The Reading of Standings
ritual.text: #congregation# gather under the scores, recited like psalms, win and loss and win.

greeting: Who do you carry this season?
The board doesn't lie, friend. People lie.
rumor: A Linesman called the {neighborhood} final to the exact point spread. Twice now.
The Saints paid out a dead man's winning ticket to his daughter. With interest.

boon.name: House Odds
boon.desc: You read games — and people — a beat faster.

religions.obligation: Honor every wager, especially the losing ones.
